description_long: This object is a stylized flower with elongated, lanceolate green leaves radiating upward from a central base. The flower features several upright, cylindrical yellow structures resembling stamens or anthers, each attached to slender white filaments. The central part of the flower is composed of a thicker, bottle-shaped green structure, possibly representing the pistil or main floral axis. The leaves and floral parts are smooth and have a matte finish, with no visible veins or surface texture. The overall form is symmetrical, with the floral elements arranged in a whorled pattern around the central axis. The coloration is primarily muted green with accents of pale yellow and white.
description: A stylized flower with upright green leaves, yellow cylindrical stamens, and a central green floral axis, arranged in a symmetrical whorled pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central green axis, three prominent yellow stamens, and symmetrical green leaves radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the central axis and the alignment of the yellow stamens and white filaments.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the green leaves and yellow stamens visible but the central axis less prominent.
description_view_3: Side view mirroring view 1, showing the arrangement of the leaves and the cylindrical yellow stamens from a different angle.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration around the central axis, with each petal radiating outward symmetrically and evenly spaced, showing no significant overlap or layering.
